asserts: bash quality/gates/agent-ux/webhook-trigger-dispatch.sh exits 0
docs/guides/dvcs-mirror-setup-template.yml exists and parses as YAML
gh api repos/reubenjohn/reposix-tokenworld-mirror/contents/.github/workflows/reposix-mirror-sync.yml returns 200 (live copy reachable)
diff -w of the two copies returns zero (byte-equal modulo whitespace)
YAML contains: repository_dispatch types=[reposix-mirror-sync], cargo binstall reposix-cli (NOT bare 'reposix'), NO github.event.client_payload references, NO set -x

rows.owner_hint: if RED: workflow YAML drifted between template and live copy; or one copy missing; or YAML structure regressed (binstall name, client_payload reference, missing fetch-depth); inspect verifier output for which structural grep failed | D-CONV-1 (2026-07-04): stripped from pre-pr -- the verifier makes a live `gh api` call against the EXTERNAL mirror repo (reubenjohn/reposix-tokenworld-mirror), which needs gh auth wired + couples every PR's CI run to that repo's current file contents and GitHub API rate limits. Unsuitable for a blocking per-PR gate as-is. Demoted to on-demand; a follow-up should either wire GITHUB_TOKEN explicitly into a dedicated job or move this to weekly/pre-release cadence (filed as a GOOD-TO-HAVE).

bus URL composition: reposix attach against an empty work tree with a pre-existing file:// origin remote bakes a `reposix::<sot>?mirror=file://...` URL into `remote.reposix.url`; `extensions.partialClone=reposix` (NOT origin); origin URL unchanged

cache materialization: REPOSIX_CACHE_DIR-rooted cache built at `<cache>/reposix/sim-demo.git/` with cache.db; reconciliation walk emits `matched=N no_id=N backend_deleted=N mirror_lag=N` line; audit_events_cache contains an attach_walk row (OP-3 unconditional)

wire-path coverage delegated to `crates/reposix-remote/tests/bus_write_happy.rs::happy_path_writes_both_refs_and_acks_ok` (helper exec + refs/mirrors writes + dual-table audit) — the shell harness asserts this test fn exists

rows.owner_hint: if RED: shell-stub assertions in dark-factory.sh dvcs-third-arm path failed -- inspect stderr for which teaching-string grep failed (?mirror=, --orphan-policy, refs/mirrors, git remote add) OR the bus URL composition / extensions.partialClone post-attach asserts failed. Wire-path failures land in the cargo test layer (bus_write_happy.rs::happy_path_writes_both_refs_and_acks_ok), not here. TokenWorld leg failures are substrate-gap-expected -- do NOT count as RED.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the shell-stub agent recovery claims were false -- e.g. the `?mirror=` teaching string, `--orphan-policy` flag, or `refs/mirrors/<sot>-synced-at` ref name were missing from reject hints or `reposix attach --help` -- the corresponding grep/--help assertions in dark-factory/dvcs-third-arm.sh would fail to match and the script would exit non-zero naming the missing string; the bus-URL composition and cache-materialization asserts likewise fail loud (wrong remote.reposix.url, wrong extensions.partialClone, or a missing attach_walk audit row) rather than silently passing.

rows.comment: RBF-FW-01 (P89 89-03) — new runner cadence `pre-release-real-backend`: env-gated (REPOSIX_ALLOWED_ORIGINS + >=1 complete sanctioned-target cred set), default-skips to NOT-VERIFIED in CI, REQUIRED at milestone-close. Per OD-2 (89-OWNER-DECISIONS.md): if this cadence cannot EXECUTE against the sanctioned target at milestone-close, the milestone-close verdict is hard RED — no owner-waiver, no until_date, no PASS-with-comment, no skip-counts-as-pass.

asserts: VALID_CADENCES contains 'pre-release-real-backend'
rows tagged with cadence skip with NOT-VERIFIED when REPOSIX_ALLOWED_ORIGINS unset
rows execute when REPOSIX_ALLOWED_ORIGINS + a credential set are present

the env-gated skip maps to NOT-VERIFIED (never PASS or WAIVED), so a milestone-close invocation of this cadence without executable creds/targets exits non-zero — hard RED per OD-2 (no waiver, no until_date, no skip-counts-as-pass)

rows.owner_hint: quality/runners/run.py + _realbackend.py (env-gate + VALID_CADENCES entry ship in 89-03)
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier asserts quality/runners/run.py VALID_CADENCES tuple contains the literal string 'pre-release-real-backend' AND that a probe row tagged with that cadence runs only when REPOSIX_ALLOWED_ORIGINS plus a complete credential set are present, skipping to status NOT-VERIFIED otherwise. If the description's claim ('the new cadence is env-gated and default-skips in CI') were false — e.g., the runner ran the verifier with no env vars set — the assertion would fail because the env-gate short-circuit would not fire and the verifier would attempt a real-backend HTTP call against a missing allowlist. Per OD-2 (89-OWNER-DECISIONS.md) the skip is NEVER a pass state: it maps to NOT-VERIFIED, so the milestone-close invocation of this cadence exits non-zero when creds/targets are missing — hard RED, no waiver, no until_date, no skip-counts-as-pass.

rows.comment: RBF-FW-02 (P89 89-04) — worked example for the new verifier kind `shell-subprocess`: drives a REAL binary subprocess (reposix preferred; bash --version as INTENTIONAL CI-portability fallback when the cargo target is absent) and emits a transcript artifact under quality/reports/transcripts/. The bash-fallback is honestly named in expected.asserts[0] — it satisfies the kind contract (subprocess + transcript), not the stronger 'invoked reposix' claim.

asserts: exits 0 against a real binary subprocess (reposix preferred; bash --version as CI fallback when cargo target absent)
transcript artifact written to quality/reports/transcripts/<slug>-<RFC3339>.txt
transcript contains argv + env_keys (NOT values) + exit_code + stdout + stderr blocks

rows.owner_hint: quality/gates/agent-ux/shell-subprocess-example.sh + lib/transcript.sh (ship in 89-04); if RED, check transcript emission under quality/reports/transcripts/
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier asserts that `bash quality/gates/agent-ux/shell-subprocess-example.sh` exits 0 AND writes a transcript file at quality/reports/transcripts/kind-shell-subprocess-worked-example-<RFC3339>.txt containing argv + env_keys (NAMES only) + cwd + exit_code + stdout + stderr blocks. The subprocess invoked is the local `reposix` binary when present (preferred); when the cargo target directory is absent (CI portability path) the verifier falls back to `bash --version` so the kind:shell-subprocess proof shape — real subprocess + transcript — is exercised regardless of whether reposix has been built. If the description's claim ('the kind drives a REAL subprocess + emits a transcript') were false — e.g., the verifier called a Python function inline rather than spawning any subprocess at all — the assertion would fail because no transcript file would exist OR the transcript would be missing the `argv:` line that proves subprocess invocation. The bash-fallback satisfies the kind contract (subprocess + transcript) but does NOT satisfy the stronger 'invoked reposix' claim; downstream P92 verifiers tightening to a real-backend invocation MUST update this row's first assert to drop the bash-fallback alternative.

rows.comment: RBF-FW-03 (P89 89-06) — milestone-close 9th probe SLOT: the dark-factory third-arm vision litmus run verbatim against TokenWorld. blast_radius P0 is load-bearing (CONTEXT D-03c): any milestone-close grading attempt while this row is NOT-VERIFIED returns exit 1, defending against the C7 self-licensing-deferral-loop. NO waiver mechanism for this row (D-03c + OD-2): a waiver block must never be added. The SLOT legitimately reads NOT-VERIFIED until P91+P92+P93+P94+P95 land the substrate; per OD-2, once the substrate exists, inability to EXECUTE this probe at milestone-close is hard RED.

asserts: agent completes vanilla-clone + reposix attach + edit + git push end-to-end against TokenWorld with zero in-context learning
audit_events_cache AND audit_events tables both have rows for the action
refs/mirrors/<sot>-{head,synced-at} advanced
transcript artifact emitted per RBF-FW-02 convention

rows.owner_hint: SLOT verifier quality/gates/agent-ux/milestone-close-vision-litmus.sh (ships in 89-06); substrate lands P91-P95; if RED after substrate ships, inspect transcript + audit_events/audit_events_cache rows + refs/mirrors advancement
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier asserts that the milestone-close 9th probe runs the dark-factory third-arm scenario against TokenWorld (or sanctioned alternative per docs/reference/testing-targets.md) AND that audit_events_cache + audit_events both contain rows for the round-trip AND that refs/mirrors/<sot>-{head,synced-at} advanced. This row WILL flip to PASS only when P91+P92+P93+P94+P95 ship their substrate; until then it legitimately returns NOT-VERIFIED. If the description's claim ('the litmus test runs verbatim against a real backend before the v0.13.0 tag') were false — e.g., the verifier silently ran against the in-process simulator — the assertion would fail because the transcript's argv would not name `reposix attach confluence::TokenWorld` and audit_events would not contain real-backend host fingerprints. Distinct from the substrate-absent SLOT state: once the substrate exists, inability to EXECUTE this probe at milestone-close (creds/targets missing or unreachable) is hard RED per OD-2 — no waiver, no until_date, no PASS-with-comment.

rows.comment: Stage-2 catalog-row minting (2026-07-04) -- anchors the QL-001 BLOCKER fix contract (push planner path-shape bugs). See .planning/milestones/v0.13.0-phases/SURPRISES-INTAKE.md 2026-07-04 entry ('discovered-by: Stage-1 Lane-A (QL-001 empirical reproduction)') for the full repro + sketched resolution that P90/P91 MUST consume. This row is EXPECTED TO FAIL once git >= 2.34 is available -- that is the honest, intentional state; on this box (git 2.25.1) the verifier's version precondition short-circuits to NOT-VERIFIED (exit 75) before any of the buggy code paths run.

asserts: a real git commit + git push from a reposix init'd working tree round-trips one edited record against the sim as a single PATCH, with zero manual fast-export and misclassification count == 0 (no spurious Create, no spurious Delete)
a no-op push (pull, no edits, push) writes zero additional backend mutations
on git < 2.34, the verifier detects the precondition gap and reports NOT-VERIFIED (exit 75) rather than FAIL -- an environment gap is not a code regression

rows.owner_hint: QL-001 BLOCKER: fix the push planner path-shape mismatch (diff.rs:104-107 vs builder.rs:90 / refresh.rs:104 / fast_import.rs:63 all spell issue paths differently) + the fast_import.rs stream-parser trailing-LF bug (156-157) + add an issues/*.md path filter (BUG-2). See SURPRISES-INTAKE.md 2026-07-04 entry for the sketched resolution P90/P91 MUST consume. | D-CONV-1 (2026-07-04): stripped from pre-pr -- a WAIVED row failing loud in every PR's CI run is noise, not signal; the QL-001 fix lands in P90/P91, at which point that phase MUST re-add pre-pr per the SURPRISES.md contract. Kept on-demand + pre-release so it's still reachable without waiting for a tag.

waiver.reason: QL-001 verified BLOCKER (real end-to-end git push reproduction, not a synthetic fast-export stream) -- the push diff planner misclassifies every record as Create+Delete due to a 4-way path-shape mismatch, and a separate stream-parser bug drops the first M-line on a genuine no-op push. The verifier exists and is genuinely correct; it fails honestly against the real bug rather than being stubbed out. This row anchors the P90/P91 fix contract -- mirrors the security/allowlist-enforcement precedent row's waiver-on-a-real-verifier pattern.

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier drives a REAL git commit+push against a real reposix-init'd tree (not a hand-built fast-export stream) and queries the sim's own audit_events table for PATCH/POST/DELETE counts. If the claim ('a real push round-trips one update with zero misclassification and a no-op push writes nothing') were false -- e.g. the planner treated an unchanged issue as Create+Delete (QL-001 BUG-1) or the no-op push's stream parser dropped an M-line and issued a spurious DELETE (BUG-3) -- the PATCH/POST/DELETE count assertions would fail because sqlite3 would report POST_COUNT>0 or DELETE_COUNT>0 or a nonzero delta on the no-op push, and the verifier would exit 1 naming the exact mismatched count.

rows.comment: RBF-FW-09 (F-K8) — a Rust #[test] fn whose name promises real/dark-factory/round-trip/e2e/push/fetch coverage but whose body neither drives a real git push/fetch subprocess nor targets a non-127.0.0.1 host nor is #[ignore] real-backend-gated is a dishonest test name. This row seeds the committed expected-RAISE baseline (R2 § C.2, 4 entries) from quality/reports/raise-list-p90.md § 2; a NEW un-baselined dishonest name (not yet in the baseline) fails the gate outright.

asserts: a Rust #[test] fn whose name matches (real|dark_factory|round_?trip|end_to_end|e2e|push|fetch) whose body neither drives a git push/fetch subprocess nor a non-127.0.0.1 host nor is #[ignore] real-backend-gated is RAISEd
the committed expected-RAISE baseline (R2 § C.2, 4 entries) matches → exit 0
a NEW un-baselined dishonest name → exit 1
per-line // test-name-honesty: ok — <reason> marker exempts, and a bare marker with no reason itself RAISEs

rows.owner_hint: quality/gates/agent-ux/test-name-vs-asserts.sh (NEW, ships 90-03) scans crates/**/*.rs for honesty-suspect #[test] fn names against the committed baseline
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier scans crates/**/*.rs for #[test] fns whose names match the honesty-suspect regex, inspects each body for a real git push/fetch subprocess invocation, a non-127.0.0.1 host reference, or a #[ignore] real-backend gate, and RAISEs (non-exempt, non-baselined) mismatches. If the description's claim ("a committed 4-entry baseline of known-dishonest names passes clean, and any NEW un-baselined dishonest name fails") were false — e.g. the scanner ignored test bodies entirely and only matched names — a synthetic fixture adding a 5th un-baselined dishonestly-named test would still exit 0, and the assertion expecting exit 1 would fail. A companion fixture asserts the per-line // test-name-honesty: ok marker exempts a flagged test, and that a bare marker with no reason string itself RAISEs (closing the silent-exemption loophole).

rows.comment: RBF-FW-10 (F-K5, consumed by P96) — the absorption-honesty spot-check template (D90-08: quality/dispatch/absorption-honesty-spot-check.md) must exist, be content-hash-bound (not merely present, since a gutted-but-present file would still pass an existence-only check), and contain the four F-K5 clauses verbatim: sample EVERY no-intake phase; spot-check author != milestone orchestrator; rubric 'walk one critical example end-to-end mentally — does it work?'; content-hash binding.

asserts: quality/dispatch/absorption-honesty-spot-check.md exists
its content sha256 matches the committed hash (content-hash binding, not mere existence)

it contains the four F-K5 clauses verbatim: sample EVERY no-intake phase / author != milestone orchestrator / rubric 'walk one critical example end-to-end mentally — does it work?' / content-hash binding

rows.owner_hint: quality/dispatch/absorption-honesty-spot-check.md (NEW, D90-08, ships 90-04) + verifier ships 90-04
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier checks the template file exists, computes its sha256 and compares against the hash recorded at grade time, and greps for the four F-K5 clauses verbatim. If the description's claim ("the template exists, is content-hash-bound, and carries all four clauses") were false — e.g. a future edit silently dropped the 'author != milestone orchestrator' clause while leaving the file present — the grep for that clause would find nothing and the assertion would fail; likewise if the file were deleted, the existence assertion fails, and if the file were edited without updating the recorded hash, the drift assertion fails.

rows.comment: RBF-FW-12 (D90-09) — a milestone-close verdict must not go GREEN unless a fresh adversarial subagent has graded the closing milestone's catalog rows and the resulting artifact (quality/reports/verifications/adversarial/<milestone>.json, D90-09 path — NOT the R1 § FW-12 milestone-adversarial/ path) reports zero failed-row audits. The gate can only DARKEN a verdict (green→red), never lighten one; it composes with compute_color/compute_exit_code rather than replacing them.

asserts: milestone-close verdict blocks GREEN (forces red) when quality/reports/verifications/adversarial/<milestone>.json is absent
blocks GREEN when the artifact reports >=1 failed row audit
the gate can only DARKEN (green->red), never lighten — composes with compute_color/compute_exit_code

rows.owner_hint: quality/runners/verdict.py gains the adversarial-artifact hook; quality/dispatch/milestone-adversarial.md (NEW, D90-09) ships 90-04
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier is a thin bash wrapper running python3 -m unittest quality.runners.test_verdict adversarial cases: an absent-artifact fixture and a failed-row-count>=1 fixture, both asserting the milestone-close verdict computes RED despite an otherwise-all-PASS catalog. If the description's claim ("milestone-close cannot go GREEN without a passing fresh adversarial audit, and the hook can only darken never lighten a verdict") were false — e.g. the hook were wired as advisory-only, or an absent artifact defaulted to a pass-through GREEN — both fixtures would observe the verdict staying GREEN and the assertions would fail. A third fixture asserts the hook cannot flip an already-RED verdict to GREEN, confirming the darken-only direction.
